title: Pan-Seared Red Snapper with Crusty Knots
description: A simple yet flavorful dish featuring pan-seared red snapper paired with crispy knots.
instructions: 2. Season the flour with salt and pepper.
3. Dredge the red snapper fillets in the flour mixture, shaking off excess.
4. Heat a skillet over medium-high heat with 2 tablespoons of oil.
5. Add the dredged snapper fillets to the skillet and sear for 2-3 minutes on each side.
6. Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 8-10 minutes, or until cooked through.
7. Meanwhile, heat the remaining 2 tablespoons of oil in a separate skillet.
8. Add the knots to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until golden brown.
9. Serve the pan-seared red snapper with the crispy knots on the side.
10. Garnish with fresh parsley, if desired.
